2605.19966.title: Detecting Fluent Optimization-Based Adversarial Prompts via Sequential Entropy Changes
2605.19966.paper_summary: Optimization-based adversarial suffixes can jailbreak aligned large language models (LLMs) while remaining fluent, weakening static and windowed perplexity-based detectors. We cast adversarial suffix detection as an online change-point detection problem over the token-level next-token entropy stream. Using the LLM system prompt to estimate a robust baseline, we standardize user-token entropies and apply a one-sided CUSUM statistic. The resulting detector, CPD Online (CPD), is model-agnostic, training-free, runs online, and localizes the adversarial suffix onset. On a benchmark of 1,012 optimization-based suffix attacks (GCG, AutoDAN, AdvPrompter, BEAST, AutoDAN-HGA) and 1,012 perplexity-controlled benign prompts, CPD improves F1 over the strongest windowed-perplexity baseline on all six open-weight chat models (LLaMA-2-7B/13B, Vicuna-7B/13B, Qwen2.5-7B/14B). On LLaMA-2-7B at the canonical CUSUM setting ($k=0$), CPD reaches AUROC $0.88$ and F1 $0.82$. Beyond prompt-level detection, CPD concentrates 79.6% of its triggers inside the adversarial suffix, versus 17-46% for windowed perplexity. Finally, when used as a lightweight gate for LLaMA Guard, CPD reduces guard calls by 17-22% on a high-volume, benign-dominated deployment while preserving guard-level detection quality
2605.19966.paper_summary_zh: 優化基於對抗的後綴可以破解對齊的大型語言模型（LLMs），同時保持流暢性，削弱靜態和基於窗口的困惑度檢測器。我們將對抗後綴檢測視為一個在線變更點檢測問題，針對標記級別的下一標記熵流。使用LLM系統提示來估算穩健的基準，我們標準化用戶標記的熵並應用單側CUSUM統計量。所得到的檢測器，CPD Online（CPD），是模型無關的、無需訓練的、在線運行的，並能定位對抗後綴的開始。在1,012個基於優化的後綴攻擊（GCG、AutoDAN、AdvPrompter、BEAST、AutoDAN-HGA）和1,012個困惑度控制的良性提示的基準上，CPD在所有六個開放權重的聊天模型（LLaMA-2-7B/13B、Vicuna-7B/13B、Qwen2.5-7B/14B）上提高了F1，相較於最強的基於窗口的困惑度基準。在LLaMA-2-7B的經典CUSUM設置下（$k=0$），CPD達到AUROC $0.88$和F1 $0.82$。超越提示級別的檢測，CPD將79.6%的觸發集中在對抗後綴內，而基於窗口的困惑度則為17-46%。最後，當作為LLaMA Guard的輕量級閘門使用時，CPD在高流量、良性主導的部署中將保護調用減少了17-22%，同時保持了保護級別的檢測質量。
2605.19966.author: Mohammed Alshaalan et.al.
2605.19966.authors: Mohammed Alshaalan, Miguel R. D. Rodrigues
